name: Australia

notes: Ultra long-haul routes usually mean multi-leg flights and early temporary-housing planning.
Allow extra buffer for jet lag and first-week admin after arrival.

commonRoutes: Sponsored employment and highly skilled migrant routes
Study permits with Dutch institution admission
Partner and family routes with relationship evidence
Entrepreneurship, startup, or self-employment where criteria are met
Working Holiday Scheme for eligible Australians
notes: Non-EU nationals generally need a recognised purpose to live in the Netherlands longer than short visits.
MVV entry visas apply in many (not all) long-stay procedures—confirm on IND and Netherlands Worldwide.

commonStarterDocuments: Birth or marriage certificates when relevant
Proof of address history where required
Permit-specific forms from sponsor or institution
countrySpecificNotes: Australian documents may require apostille or authentication before foreign authorities accept them (see Smartraveller).
State and Commonwealth issuing bodies can have different lead times.

notes: Compare shared container, full container, and air freight for essentials only.
Align delivery with lease start and temporary accommodation.

heroImageAlt: A peaceful Dutch canal lined with traditional gabled houses and a bridge—an arrival-in-the-Netherlands mood for Australians planning a long-haul move.

seo.title: Moving to the Netherlands from Australia | Visas, Documents, Costs & Practical Setup
seo.description: Planning a move from Australia to the Netherlands? Explore visa routes, MVV guidance, document prep, working holiday options, costs, and the practical steps to settle in.
keywords: moving to the netherlands from australia

faqOverrides.q: Do Australians need a visa to live in the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: For stays longer than short visits, most Australians need a Dutch residence permit for a recognised purpose such as work, study, family, or certain exchange routes. Whether you also need an MVV (entry visa) depends on your nationality and permit type—confirm on IND and Netherlands Worldwide.

faqOverrides.q: What is an MVV for the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: The MVV is a provisional residence permit used as an entry visa in many long-stay procedures. It can let you travel to the Netherlands to complete steps such as collecting your residence permit when your route requires it. Check IND pages and the Australia-specific Netherlands Worldwide instructions.

faqOverrides.q: Where do Australians apply for the MVV sticker?
faqOverrides.a: Netherlands Worldwide describes how to apply for the MVV long-stay visa from Australia. Follow the current country-specific page for submission details.

faqOverrides.q: Can Australians use a Dutch working holiday?
faqOverrides.a: Yes—Australians can be eligible for the Netherlands Working Holiday Programme / working holiday residence permit under the conditions published by Netherlands Worldwide and the IND. The IND also explains how to apply, including online application where it applies. It is not the same as standard long-term skilled employment.

faqOverrides.q: How should I prepare Australian documents for Dutch authorities?
faqOverrides.a: Smartraveller and other Australian government guidance explain that documents may need apostille or authentication before foreign authorities accept them. Dutch procedures may also require sworn translation. Check the document list for your specific permit or municipality process.

faqOverrides.q: Where do Australians get consular help in the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: The Australian Embassy in the Netherlands publishes official country and consular information for Australians living in or travelling to the Netherlands.

customNotes: Long-haul moves often pair temporary housing, sea freight, and cross-border banking early in the plan.
